domain_signal.summary: Project management tool for software teams; design-led, API-first, recently focused on agent-native workflows.

domain_evidence.claim: Active changelog with entries every 1-2 weeks
Co-founder publicly framing Linear as agent-native PM tool
Demo repos (claude-managed-agents-demo, weather-bot) for agent-Linear integration

apis.notes: Schema is introspectable. No REST surface.

sdks.notes: Primary SDK; codegen tooling available for other languages but no official non-TS SDK.

engineering_practices.notes: Linear's GitHub footprint is small and product-focused — they're not an OSS-publishing company. This is a signal in itself: tightly scoped public surface.

company_ai_posture.notes: Linear ships Linear Agent + MCP as product features. Not a research org — agentic posture is product-integration, not foundation-model work.

agentic_landscape.competition_assessment: Official MCP covers issue/project/comment CRUD. A Tessl skill that just wraps the API is redundant. Differentiation must come from higher-level workflows (sprint planning, triage, release tracking) that compose multiple primitives — i.e. workflow_skill, not api_wrapper.

people_signal.name: Tuomas Artman
people_signal.current_focus: Engineering systems; recent Pragmatic Engineer feature on Linear's architecture

barriers: free signup; capped at 2 teams / 250 issues on free tier

skill_targets.title: Linear sprint and release workflow recipes
skill_targets.rationale: Official MCP covers CRUD. Agents using Linear typically need workflow-level help: 'plan a sprint from this backlog', 'triage these incoming issues', 'cut a release and update tracking issues'. Current scenarios are not well-served by raw API access.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Compose MCP primitives into named multi-step workflows; ship eval scenarios that prove lift over raw MCP usage.

skill_targets.title: Linear GraphQL query construction helper
skill_targets.rationale: GraphQL has high learning curve for agents that haven't seen the schema before; a skill could teach query construction patterns specific to Linear's schema (connections, filters, pagination).
skill_targets.existing_competition: Official MCP makes most queries unnecessary; @linear/sdk wraps common ones.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Useful only when agent needs to escape MCP/SDK and hit raw GraphQL — narrow window.

skill_targets.title: Linear Agent SDK skill
skill_targets.rationale: Linear shipped a dedicated Agents SDK with its own auth flow. Building agents *for* Linear (not just *using* Linear from agents) is a distinct, currently-novel use case with a Claude integration demo already published.
skill_targets.existing_competition: None at the skill layer — only Linear's own demo repo.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: First skill teaching the Linear Agents SDK lifecycle (auth, webhooks, response patterns).
